cards.grammarNote: Regular -ER verb with spelling change: manger → nous mangeons (adds e before -ons to preserve the soft g sound).
Irregular verb. je bois, tu bois, il boit, nous buvons, vous buvez, ils boivent.
Regular -ER verb. j'aime, tu aimes, il aime. aimer + infinitive = to like doing: j'aime manger.
French has no separate present progressive — je mange means both 'I eat' and 'I am eating'.
de l' is the partitive article before vowels — used for uncountable quantities: de l'eau, du vin, de la soupe.
On = we (informal spoken). On commande is more natural than nous commandons in casual speech.
Je voudrais (I would like) is the polite way to order — more formal than je veux (I want).
Goûte is the informal tu imperative. Formal: Goûtez ça. ça = that/this (informal demonstrative).

cards.back: Je ne mange pas de viande.
cards.grammarNote: After negation, un/une/du/de la/de l' all become de: je mange du pain → je ne mange pas de pain.
